title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss set an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in a single season after being traded from the Raiders to the Patriots.
Wes Welker became one of the most prolific slot receivers in the NFL after joining the Patriots from the Dolphins in 2007.
Josh Gordon showed potential as a No. 1 receiver with over 700 yards in 2018 but was hampered by off-field issues.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected in the eighth inning for arguing a strike three call during a 6-5 loss to the Reds.
Cora expressed disappointment that umpire C.B. Bucknor didn't defer to the first base umpire on Trevor Story's check swing.
Roman Anthony criticized his own decision to challenge a pitch call, which left the Red Sox without further challenge options.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire CB Bucknor for a strike three call that led to Alex Cora's ejection during a Red Sox game.
Story's strikeout in the late game against a pitch ruled a swing was controversial, though the Red Sox eventually tied before losing.
The Red Sox lost 6-5 to their opponents despite home runs by Trevor Story and Wilyer Abreu, putting them at a 1-1 record.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha has scored 11 goals and 19 points in 16 games since the Bruins returned from the Olympic break.
Zacha was unable to participate in the Olympics due to a concussion, missing his chance to represent Czechia.
Zacha, 28, has set a career-high with 26 goals this season, close to surpassing his points record with Boston.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray struggled in his Red Sox debut, allowing four runs over four innings against the Cincinnati Reds.
Manager Alex Cora noted Gray's improvement after the first inning and praised the bullpen's strong performance.
Boston Red Sox lost 6-5 to the Reds in an 11-inning game; Gray's next start is on April 3.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Thousands rallied at Boston Common for the third 'No Kings' protest, part of a nation-wide series of demonstrations.
The protest featured speeches from Massachusetts leaders including Gov. Maura Healey and performances by the Dropkick Murphys.
Organizers set up action tables around the Common for attendees to join groups like the ACLU, focusing on civic engagement.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute, 17-song set at Boston's House of Blues, featuring theatrical shock rock and fake blood.
Lead singer Michael Bishop and band wore gothic costumes, delivering a performance heavy with social and political satire.
The setlist included songs like 'Tormentor,' 'Lot Lizard,' and a cover of Chappell Roan's 'Pink Pony Club.'

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Four Celtics players, including stars Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum, are questionable for Sunday's game against Charlotte.
Jaylen Brown is dealing with left Achilles tendinitis, while Tatum manages right Achilles repair recovery.
Nikola Vucevic remains out with a right ring finger fracture for the Celtics' upcoming game.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered at Boston Common for 'No Kings' protest, part of over 160 rallies across Massachusetts.
Prominent speakers included Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Ed Markey, and Sen. Elizabeth Warren, with performances by Dropkick Murphys.
Veterans, church members, and costumed participants attended, voicing concerns about democracy and the country's direction.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered at Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest on March 28, part of a national movement.
Massachusetts hosted over 160 'No Kings' events, with significant participation in Boston.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the Boston protest participants.
